attempt: Transfer TICKET47's rule: a bounded state memory that closes on a positive-pressure lasso cannot be used as a proof object. Either prove the lasso is unreachable in the real system or use a stronger invariant whose state does not merely remember a bounded suffix.

bounded_result.discarded_shortcut: bounded zero-state memory that repeats on an off-critical kernel lasso
bounded_result.retained_target: A kernel-state theorem proves every off-critical lasso is unreachable or strictly positivity-increasing.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: an off-critical zero-pressure lasso that returns every bounded kernel memory to the same state

obstruction: The transfer is a proof-search discipline rather than a theorem for the target problem. It blocks periodic finite-memory repairs but leaves the infinite bridge open.
candidate_theorem: A kernel-state theorem proves every off-critical lasso is unreachable or strictly positivity-increasing.
next_experiment: Search for zero-kernel lassos before accepting any bounded-memory zero exclusion.
claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.
